skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate songs, jingles, loops, and instrumental music with ElevenLabs Music through the RunComfy CLI. The skill explains prompt structure, duration settings, output formats, pricing considerations, authentication, and generated audio downloads.
skill.summary: Create structured vocal or instrumental music with ElevenLabs Music through RunComfy.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows the file is a Markdown skill guide, not executable bundled code. The confirmed risks are legitimate for the skill purpose: it invokes the RunComfy CLI, sends user prompts to RunComfy network endpoints, writes generated audio, and may use a local auth token. Weak crypto, shell substitution, and system reconnaissance alerts appear to be false positives from documentation text and inline examples.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Invocation Required
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run `runcomfy run elevenlabs/elevenlabs/music-generation` and includes setup commands for npm or npx. This is expected behavior, but it gives the skill an external command execution dependency and should be limited to trusted RunComfy CLI commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata and examples clearly show intended Bash use with the RunComfy CLI. The commands are documented and scoped, so the risk is real but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Network Calls to RunComfy Services
medium_findings.description: The documented workflow sends music prompts and request data to RunComfy Model API endpoints and downloads generated audio from RunComfy domains. This is necessary for the cloud music-generation service, but users should treat prompts and lyrics as data shared with a third party.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes API submission, polling, and audio download behavior. The endpoints are vendor domains, and no unrelated exfiltration destination was found.

medium_findings.title: Local Token and Output File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents `runcomfy login`, token storage at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json`, use of the `RUNCOMFY_TOKEN` environment variable, and output downloads to `--output-dir`. This is legitimate credential and output handling, but it requires careful permission and logging controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly states where the CLI stores tokens and where generated files are downloaded. The same section warns not to echo or log tokens, which reduces but does not remove operational risk.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Text
low_findings.description: Many reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptographic algorithm, command substitution, and reconnaissance detections occur inside Markdown prose, inline code, tables, or examples. No Ruby code, cryptographic implementation, or system reconnaissance behavior was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a single Markdown skill description. The suspicious tokens are documentation examples such as backticks, `music_length_ms`, `$(...)`, and exit-code tables rather than executable logic.

low_findings.title: Remote Install Guidance Requires User Review
low_findings.description: The security section warns agents not to pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell and says operators should review any curl-pipe install path first. This is a positive safety instruction, but the topic is still relevant because users may follow linked installation documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a defensive warning rather than an unsafe instruction. It still touches a sensitive installation pattern, so it is worth preserving as a marketplace warning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access for RunComfy Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants `Bash(runcomfy *)` and relies on local CLI execution. This command scope matches the skill purpose, but marketplace users should understand that it executes local commands and depends on the installed CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command scope is declared in metadata and repeated in examples. It is specific to RunComfy, not arbitrary shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party API Submission and Download Flow
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow sends prompt content to RunComfy and downloads generated audio files. This is required for functionality, but it creates normal third-party data sharing and file download risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The security section explicitly describes POST, polling, allowlisted endpoints, and a file size cap. This confirms the behavior while showing some built-in guardrails.

content.value_statement: Music prompts often need clear structure, duration control, and cost awareness. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create ElevenLabs Music requests through RunComfy.
actual_capabilities: Guides generation of vocal songs with section markers like Intro, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, and Outro.
Supports instrumental tracks by using the `force_instrumental` option and no-vocal prompt guidance.
Documents duration control from 5 seconds to 5 minutes using `music_length_ms`.
Shows RunComfy CLI commands for full songs, background beds, and short brand jingles.
Explains output formats, generated audio downloads, pricing estimates, and common CLI exit codes.
Includes security notes for token storage, shell input boundaries, and allowed outbound endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy login or token before generation can run.
Sends prompt text and lyrics to RunComfy and ElevenLabs-backed cloud services.
Does not provide sound effects, text-to-speech, voice cloning, or singer identity cloning.
Limits each generated track to 5 seconds through 5 minutes per API call.

use_cases.title: Podcast Intro Music
use_cases.description: Create short instrumental intro and outro tracks with controlled duration, mood, and clean endings.

use_cases.title: Brand Jingles
use_cases.description: Generate five to thirty second stingers or jingles with clear instrumentation and commercial-friendly direction.

use_cases.title: Game Background Loops
use_cases.description: Draft loop-friendly instrumental beds with tempo, genre, and groove instructions for gameplay scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Instrumental
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 30 second instrumental background track with ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy. Use a calm lo-fi style, warm piano, soft drums, and no vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Podcast Intro
prompt_templates.scenario: Short branded audio for a show.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 15 second podcast intro. Use upbeat electronic music, a clear opening, a clean ending, and no vocals. Keep the prompt ready for RunComfy.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Structured Song Prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 60 second pop song prompt with a style brief and section markers for Intro, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, and Outro. Include original lyrics.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Iterative Music Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with cost and revision control.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me generate a 45 second draft first, then a 3 minute final version. Define genre, BPM, instruments, vocal direction, section markers, and RunComfy settings.

output_examples.input: A creator asks for a short upbeat intro for a technology podcast.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a concise RunComfy-ready music brief with duration, mood, instruments, no-vocal guidance, and a clean ending.

output_examples.input: A game developer needs a loop-friendly background track for a calm puzzle level.
output_examples.output: The skill creates an instrumental prompt with tempo, texture, looping language, duration guidance, and file output expectations.

output_examples.input: A musician wants a one minute demo with lyrics and clear song sections.
output_examples.output: The skill drafts an ElevenLabs Music prompt that combines style, vocal direction, section markers, and original lyric lines.

best_practices: Start with a short draft before generating a longer and more expensive final track.
Put genre, mood, BPM, instruments, vocals, and structure in one clear prompt field.
Use original lyrics or confirm rights before generating music from supplied lyrics.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API tokens, private lyrics, or unreleased campaign secrets into prompts.
Do not mix conflicting genres or moods in the same generation request.
Do not use this skill for sound effects, speech synthesis, or voice cloning tasks.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate full songs, instrumental beds, jingles, podcast intros, and game loops through ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy.

faq.question: Does it require an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses the local RunComfy CLI, which submits requests to RunComfy cloud endpoints.

faq.question: Can it create instrumental-only music?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents `force_instrumental: true` and recommends also saying no vocals in the prompt.

faq.question: How long can each track be?
faq.answer: The documented range is 5 seconds to 5 minutes per generation request.

faq.question: Can it clone a singer or voice?
faq.answer: No. The skill says this endpoint does not request specific voice identities or clone singers.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing generated music?
faq.answer: Users should review prompt privacy, lyric rights, commercial terms, output quality, and any required attribution or licensing terms.
